About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: momentum-ranker Today's data reveals two exceptional performers at opposite momentum extremes. INVX claims the #1 ranking with a composite score of 87.9, driven by exceptional ratio consistency (94.5/100) and resilience on down SPY days (92.9/100), though its 1-month return of 1.42% lags its broader momentum narrative of 84.3% annual gains. SYRE presents a far more volatile picture with a stunning 317.84% 1-year return and 223.16% 6-month gain, yet shows dangerous momentum deterioration with a deeply negative ratio_1w_1d of -14.53 and a concerning bad_spy_score of -0.4462, indicating it actually declines when the broader market struggles—a red flag for trend sustainability. The contrast is stark: INVX demonstrates clean, consistent momentum with tight correlations to tech (QQQ at 0.313), while SYRE's explosive gains appear concentrated in longer timeframes with weaker near-term ratio progression and higher fragility. Both pass all SMA gates, but INVX's balanced outperformance across multiple timeframes suggests more stable shorter-term momentum, whereas SYRE's extreme skew toward longer-term returns masked by recent weakness warrants caution about timing and trend reliability.
